A model attribution edit summary is `Content in this edit is translated from the existing German Wikipedia article at [[:de:Mariensäule (München)]]; see its history for attribution.` * You may also add the template `{{Translated|de|Mariensäule (München)}}` to the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Mariens%C3%A4ule "Talk:Mariensäule"). * For more guidance, see [Wikipedia:Translation](/wiki/Wikipedia:Translation "Wikipedia:Translation"). |

(lit. 'Mary's Column') is a [Marian column](/wiki/Marian_and_Holy_Trinity_columns "Marian and Holy Trinity columns") located on the [Marienplatz](/wiki/Marienplatz "Marienplatz") in [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Mary is revered here as *[Patrona Bavariae](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patrona_Bavariae "de:Patrona Bavariae")* (Latin: Protector of [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria")).
For more and detailed pictures of the column see the respective [German language article](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariens%C3%A4ule_(M%C3%BCnchen) "de:Mariensäule (München)") on wikipedia.de.

outgoing_links.text: Duke Elector Maximilian I of Bavaria

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/36/Marienstatue_-_panoramio_%281%29.jpg/250px-Marienstatue_-_panoramio_%281%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Marienstatue_-_panoramio_(1).jpg)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f2/Muenchen_Mariensaule_01.jpg/250px-Muenchen_Mariensaule_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Muenchen_Mariensaule_01.jpg)

Virgin Mary atop the Mariensäule.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ca/Muenchen_Mariensaule_Fabeltier_Drache.JPG/250px-Muenchen_Mariensaule_Fabeltier_Drache.JPG)](/wiki/File:Muenchen_Mariensaule_Fabeltier_Drache.JPG)

Putto fighting a dragon.

The **Mariensäule** (lit. 'Mary's Column') is a [Marian column](/wiki/Marian_and_Holy_Trinity_columns "Marian and Holy Trinity columns") located on the [Marienplatz](/wiki/Marienplatz "Marienplatz") in [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Mary is revered here as *[Patrona Bavariae](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patrona_Bavariae "de:Patrona Bavariae")* (Latin: Protector of [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria")).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mariens%C3%A4ule&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

It was erected in 1638 to celebrate the end of [Swedish](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") occupation during the [Thirty Years' War](/wiki/Thirty_Years%27_War "Thirty Years' War"), following a vow by [Duke Elector Maximilian I of Bavaria](/wiki/Maximilian_I,_Elector_of_Bavaria "Maximilian I, Elector of Bavaria") if the ducal residential cities of Munich and [Landshut](/wiki/Landshut "Landshut") would be spared from war destruction. The column is topped by a [golden](/wiki/Gold "Gold") [statue](/wiki/Statue "Statue") of the [Virgin Mary](/wiki/Mary_(mother_of_Jesus) "Mary (mother of Jesus)") standing on a crescent moon as the [Queen of Heaven](/wiki/Queen_of_Heaven "Queen of Heaven"), created in 1590. The figure was originally located in the [Frauenkirche](/wiki/Munich_Frauenkirche "Munich Frauenkirche"). *Mariensäule* in Munich was the first column of this type built north of the [Alps](/wiki/Alps "Alps") and inspired erecting other Marian columns in this part of Europe.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mariens%C3%A4ule&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Features")]

At each corner of the column's pedestal is a statue of a [putto](/wiki/Putto "Putto"), created by [Ferdinand Murmann](/w/index.php?title=Ferdinand_Murmann&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ferdinand Murmann (page does not exist)"). The four putti are each depicted fighting a different beast, symbolizing the city's overcoming of adversities: war represented by the lion, pestilence by the [cockatrice](/wiki/Cockatrice "Cockatrice"), hunger or famine by the [dragon](/wiki/Dragon "Dragon") and [heresy](/wiki/Heresy "Heresy") by the serpent.

The full inscription is as follows (with a translation)

| Latin | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| DEO OPTIMO MAXIMO  VIRGINI DEIPARAE  BOICAE DOMINAE  BENIGNISSIMAE  PROTECTRICI  POTENTISSIMAE  OB PATRIAM  VRBES EXERCITVS  SEIPSVM DOMVM  ET SPES SVAS  SERVATAS  HOC PERENNE  AD POSTEROS  MONVMENTVM  MAXIMILIANVS  COM. PAL. RHENI  VTRIVSQVE BAVARIAE DVX  S.R.I. ARCHIDAP.  ET ELECTOR  CLIENTVM INFIMVS  GRATVS SVPPLEX  POSUIT A. MDCXXXIIX | TO GOD THE BEST AND GREATEST  AND TO THE VIRGIN, THE MOTHER OF GOD  MISTRESS OF BAVARIA  MOST BENIGN  PROTECTRESS  MOST POWERFUL;  BECAUSE THE HOMELAND  CITIES, ARMIES  HIS OWN HOUSE  AND HIS HOPES  WERE SAVED  THIS LASTING  MEMORIAL  FOR THOSE TO COME  MAXIMILIANVS   PALSGRAVE OF THE RHINE  RULER OF BOTH BAVARIAS  ARCHSTEWARD OF THE HOLY ROMAN EMPIRE  AND ELECTOR  THE WORST OF HER SERVANTS  A GRATEFUL SUPPLICANT  PUT UP IN THE YEAR 1638 |
